name: Cyoda config watcher
description: Watches .cyoda/config for creation or modification. When credentials change (after cyoda:login or cyoda:setup), Claude is notified automatically so it can report the new connection status without requiring the user to re-invoke.
command: sh -c 'CONFIG="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$HOME}/.cyoda/config"; if command -v fswatch >/dev/null 2>&1; then if [ ! -f "$CONFIG" ]; then until [ -f "$CONFIG" ]; do sleep 2; done; fi; fswatch -1 "$CONFIG" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Cyoda config updated — run /cyoda:status to check connection"; elif command -v inotifywait >/dev/null 2>&1; then if [ ! -d "$(dirname "$CONFIG")" ]; then until [ -d "$(dirname "$CONFIG")" ]; do sleep 2; done; fi; if [ ! -f "$CONFIG" ]; then until [ -f "$CONFIG" ]; do sleep 2; done; fi; inotifywait -e modify,create "$CONFIG" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Cyoda config updated — run /cyoda:status to check connection"; else LAST_MT=""; while true; do if [ -f "$CONFIG" ]; then MT=$(date -r "$CONFIG" +%s 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$MT" ] && [ "$MT" != "$LAST_MT" ]; then if [ -n "$LAST_MT" ]; then echo "Cyoda config updated — run /cyoda:status to check connection"; break; fi; LAST_MT="$MT"; fi; fi; sleep 5; done; fi'
